dsl_rule.translation_notes: Strategy uses z-score of a pairs spread (cl1 - hedgeRatio*cl2). Mapped to zscore_20 as the closest available z-score indicator. Long entry when zscore ≤ -2, short entry when zscore ≥ +2, exit when |zscore| ≤ 1 (mean reversion threshold). The exit condition for both directions requires zscore to be within ±1 standard deviations. Default stop loss and hold period applied as parameters were marked AUTHOR_SILENT.

backtest_rule.reason: Strategy uses z-score of a pairs spread (cl1 - hedgeRatio*cl2). Mapped to zscore_20 as the closest available z-score indicator. Long entry when zscore ≤ -2, short entry when zscore ≥ +2, exit when |zscore| ≤ 1 (mean reversion threshold). The exit condition for both directions requires zscore to be within ±1 standard deviations. Default stop loss and hold period applied as parameters were marked AUTHOR_SILENT.

source_rule_text: A mean-reversion pairs trading strategy that goes long the spread when it drops below -2 standard deviations and goes short when it rises above +2 standard deviations, exiting when it mean-reverts within ±1 standard deviation.
